title: Master i-Constructibility Report

S61-S69.title: Initial Setup & Lean Battlefield
results: N=1..9 exhaustive search (1429 trees), no i witness
iπ-closure fails: Im/π irrational in general
Lean battlefield: depth hierarchy formalized
GrandSynthesis.lean: eml0_strict_eml1 proved
Sorry count: 11 (after S69)

S70-S79.title: i-Constructibility Sprint
results: T19 PROVED: strict grammar i-barrier, 3-line induction
Extended closure: N=1..7, first complex Im=-pi at N=5
iπ algebra: exp(iπ)=-1, Log(-1)=iπ, closed loop
tan(1) obstruction identified: Im=1 requires arg(y)=-1
StrictBarrier.lean: 0 sorries
ExtendedClosure.lean: framework, 3 sorries
Private repo v3.1.0 tagged

S80-S89.title: tan(1) Non-Membership Attack Block
results: S80: Canonical Claim C formalized (tan(1) ∉ T)
S81: Transcendence reduction — Strategy D (structural) recommended
S82: Nesterenko analysis — useful once structure known, not direct
S83: Gelfond-Schneider — inapplicable directly; Schanuel connection
S84: PSLQ at 300 digits — NO relation tan(1) ∈ {pi, e, ln(2)}
S85: 50,907 depth-5 values computed — no arg = ±1 found
S86: Branch-cut bounding — Im=1 iff arg(y)=-1 (self-similar reduction)
S87: Lean stub — tan1_not_in_eml_ratio_set, eml1_arg_ne_one (2 sorries)
S88: Literature bridge — Schanuel would close gap; structural path open

theorem_registry.name: Strict-Grammar i-Barrier
theorem_registry.statement: Under strict grammar, all EML values are real; i not constructible

theorem_registry.name: Extended-Grammar i-Conjecture
theorem_registry.statement: i not constructible from {1} under extended principal-branch EML
evidence: 50,907 values at depth ≤ 5 computed — no witness
PSLQ at 300 digits — no relation found
Self-similar reduction: claim reduces to itself at each depth

claim_C.name: tan(1) Non-Membership
claim_C.statement: tan(1) ∉ { Im(z)/Re(z) : z ∈ EML_1, Re(z)≠0, Im(z)≠0 }
claim_C.evidence: 50,907 values, PSLQ 300 digits, no counterexample found
claim_C.gap: Proof requires Schanuel or novel structural theorem

open_questions: Is Im(EML_1) ⊆ (-pi, 0] ∪ {0}? (Structural bound — would close the gap partially)
Is Re(x) ≥ 1 for all x ∈ EML_1? (Would constrain Im-part growth)
Is T_i provable via Schanuel? (Major open problem in transcendence theory)
Can tan(1) be expressed in terms of {pi, e, ln(2)}? (PSLQ says no at 300 digits)
What is the full transcendence degree of the field generated by EML_1 over Q?
next_sprint_recommendation: S90-S99: EML Atlas expansion (10 new domains) + structural Im-part bound attempt. Target: prove Im(EML_1) ⊆ (-pi, 0] at depth ≤ k for all k, using careful induction. If successful: T_i proven. If failed: identify counterexample depth.
